# 🚀 Build One Feature. Become a Founding Builder.

First of all...

**Thank you for opening this GitHub Issue. ❤️**

The fact that you're reading this already means a lot to us.

We are **Kevin & Micha**, the founders of **Bankrupt to 1 Million**.

We are documenting our journey from financial rock bottom while trying to build something much bigger than ourselves.

Not just a website.

Not just a project.

A movement.

Instead of waiting until we have funding or a large development team, we have decided to build this platform together with people from around the world.

One feature at a time.

> **Many hands make light work.**

# 🎨 Design Reference

<!-- Add approved mockups, screenshots, wireframes or gallery links. -->

-

## Important design rule

When an approved mockup or screenshot is attached, treat it as the **visual Source of Truth**.

Do not redesign the feature from scratch.

Do not replace the supplied design with a generic template.

Only make changes required for:

- responsiveness;
- accessibility;
- technical feasibility;
- consistency with the existing codebase.

Any substantial design deviation should be discussed in this issue first.

---

# ♿ Accessibility Requirements

The implementation must include:

- semantic HTML where applicable;
- accessible labels for inputs and controls;
- keyboard navigation;
- visible focus states;
- sufficient colour contrast;
- descriptive button text;
- useful alt text for meaningful images;
- accessible validation and error messages.

---

# 🧠 Frontend Behaviour

<!-- Specify local state, interactions and visual states. -->

Include where relevant:

- default state;
- hover state;
- focus state;
- active state;
- disabled state;
- loading state;
- empty state;
- error state;
- success state.

---

# ✅ Validation

<!-- Define validation rules if the feature contains a form. -->

Validate:

- required fields;
- URLs;
- character limits;
- file types and sizes;
- permissions or consent;
- any feature-specific rules.

Show validation inline.

Do not use browser alert popups.

---

# 🧩 Component Requirements

Create reusable components where appropriate.

Possible examples:

- `FormSection`
- `FormField`
- `SelectField`
- `TextAreaField`
- `ImageUpload`
- `SectionNavigation`
- `PreviewCard`
- `SuccessState`

Use existing components and design tokens whenever possible.

Do not duplicate existing UI unnecessarily.

---

For this issue:

- [ ] Frontend only
- [ ] Mock or local data only
- [ ] Backend integration required
- [ ] Database integration required
- [ ] Authentication required
- [ ] External API required

---

# 🚫 Out of Scope

Do not add unless explicitly required:

- unrelated pages;
- unrelated redesigns;
- new authentication;
- new database architecture;
- new payment systems;
- new external services;
- unnecessary dependencies;
- speculative features;
- major refactors outside this issue.

Additional out-of-scope items:

- 
- 

---

# ✅ Acceptance Criteria

The issue is complete when:

- [ ] The supplied design is implemented faithfully.
- [ ] All required content and functionality are present.
- [ ] Desktop behaviour works.
- [ ] Tablet behaviour works.
- [ ] Mobile behaviour works.
- [ ] Required validation works.
- [ ] Loading, error and success states are implemented where applicable.
- [ ] Keyboard navigation works.
- [ ] Existing components and design tokens are reused where possible.
- [ ] No unrelated functionality was changed.
- [ ] No new critical console errors were introduced.
- [ ] No new critical lint, type-check or build errors were introduced.
- [ ] The Pull Request includes screenshots or a short video.

Add feature-specific criteria:

- [ ] 
- [ ] 
- [ ] 

---

# 🧪 Verification

Before opening the Pull Request:

- test the complete user flow;
- test required fields and invalid input;
- test all interactive controls;
- test desktop, tablet and mobile layouts;
- test keyboard navigation;
- check the browser console;
- run the project's existing lint command;
- run the project's existing type-check command;
- run the project's existing tests;
- run the production build where applicable.

Document anything that could not be verified.

---

# 📸 Pull Request Requirements

The Pull Request must include:

- a concise implementation summary;
- a reference to this issue;
- desktop screenshot;
- mobile screenshot;
- testing performed;
- known limitations;
- follow-up work, if needed.

Use:

```text
Closes #ISSUE_NUMBER
```

---

# ⏱ Difficulty and Estimated Time

## Difficulty

- [ ] 🟢 Beginner
- [ ] 🟡 Intermediate
- [ ] 🔴 Advanced

## Estimated time

- [ ] Under 1 hour
- [ ] 1–2 hours
- [ ] 2–4 hours
- [ ] 4–8 hours
- [ ] More than 8 hours

This estimate is only guidance. Quality and honest communication matter more than speed.

---

# 🙋 Claim This Issue

Comment below:

```text
I'd like to work on this.
```

Please wait until the issue is assigned to you before beginning work.

This prevents multiple contributors from unknowingly building the same feature.

---

# 🌟 Become a Founding Builder

Every merged contribution becomes part of the history of **Bankrupt to 1 Million**.

After your Pull Request is merged, you will be invited to complete a **Founding Builder Profile**.

With your permission, the future Founding Builders Wall may showcase:

- profile photo;
- full name or preferred display name;
- developer or contributor role;
- country or location;
- GitHub profile;
- portfolio and social links;
- short biography;
- personal support message;
- completed GitHub Issues;
- features contributed;
- date joined.

Your work will not disappear into a repository.

**It will become part of the story.**

---

# ❤️ A Personal Thank You

Building a movement is not about one founder doing everything.

It is about people choosing to believe before success is guaranteed.

Whether this is your first open-source contribution or your hundredth, thank you.

Thank you for your time.

Thank you for your talent.

Thank you for helping us prove that meaningful things can still be built when the right people decide to help one another.

With sincere gratitude,

**Kevin & Micha**  
Founders of Bankrupt to 1 Million

> **Many hands make light work.** ❤️🚀
